back: Selection is when a program chooses which instructions to run based on a condition.

front: What does an IF statement do?
back: An IF statement runs instructions only if a condition is true.
A condition is a test that evaluates to TRUE or FALSE.
ELSE gives instructions to run when the IF condition is false.

front: What keyword closes an IF statement?

back: Nested selection is when an IF statement is placed inside another IF statement.

front: What does = mean in a condition?
What does <> mean in a condition?

back: AND returns TRUE only if both conditions are true.
OR returns TRUE if at least one condition is true.
NOT reverses the result of a condition.

front: What does a CASE statement do?
back: A CASE statement chooses one branch based on the value of one variable.

front: When is a CASE statement useful?
back: When there are several fixed values to choose from.

front: What does OTHERWISE do in a CASE statement?
back: OTHERWISE runs if none of the previous CASE values match.

front: What keyword closes a CASE statement?
